locked_parameters.observable_distribution_P: law of (Z,S,Y,X), where Z in {0,1} is randomized, S in {0,1,2} for the flagship K=3 cell and may lie in {0,...,K-1} in reductions, Y in finite support Ygrid subset [0,1], and X in finite support Xgrid
locked_parameters.parameter_theta: theta_g = mu_1g - mu_0g for a fixed principal stratum g=(s0,s1), with mu_zg=E[Y(z)|G=g] and G=(S(0),S(1))
locked_parameters.identifying_restrictions: randomization, consistency, positivity, supplied covariate-conditional ordinal principal scores pi_g(x), simplex and principal-score calibration of posterior stratum weights q_{z,g}(x,y), and Gamma-adjacent odds-ratio divergence inside each observed (Z=z,S=k,X=x,Y=y) cell
locked_parameters.bounding_functional: the common-law feasible polytope Q(P,pi,Gamma), its K=3 middle-facet certificate C_mid(P,pi,Gamma), the ordinal sharp interval [L_g(P,pi,Gamma),U_g(P,pi,Gamma)], and the symmetric witness slack Delta_mid_sym(Gamma) that certifies the non-PI phase Gamma>1 by comparing two active-basis endpoint formulas derived from the same K=3 cell primitives

named_assumptions.label: ass:positivity
named_assumptions.statement: \mathcal X, \mathcal Y, and the principal-strata set are finite, \mathcal Y\subset[0,1], 0<e_z<1, and p_g=\E\{\pi_g(X)\}>0 for the target stratum. Every conditional probability, posterior coordinate, and odds ratio below is evaluated only on positive-probability observed cells.

named_assumptions.label: ass:principal-scores
named_assumptions.statement: For each x, \pi(x) is a probability vector on principal strata and is compatible with the observed intermediate margins: \Pr(S=k\mid Z=z,X=x)=\sum_{h\in A_z(k)}\pi_h(x).

named_assumptions.label: ass:divergence
named_assumptions.statement: Fix \Gamma\in[1,\infty]. A posterior array q is feasible when, in every positive observed cell (z,k,x,y), it satisfies simplex, principal-score calibration, and the adjacent odds-ratio inequalities \Gamma^{-1}q_{z,\ell}(x,y)w_h^z(x) \le q_{z,h}(x,y)w_\ell^z(x) \le \Gamma q_{z,\ell}(x,y)w_h^z(x) for every adjacent pair h\sim_z \ell in that cell.

named_assumptions.label: ass:witness-cell
named_assumptions.statement: For the flagship certificate, there is one positive cell (z,k,x_0) with three adjacent strata a\sim_z b\sim_z c, local outcome support \{0,1\}, \Pr(Y=1\mid Z=z,S=k,X=x_0)=1/2, equal baseline weights w_a=w_b=w_c=1/3, and finite sensitivity radius 1<\Gamma<\infty.

named_assumptions.label: ass:phase-neighborhood
named_assumptions.statement: At the witness point, every simplex, calibration, positivity, and margin-compatibility constraint that is not active under the candidate coherent and pairwise endpoint bases has strictly positive slack, so those active bases persist under sufficiently small relative perturbations of (P,\pi) inside the margin-compatible parameter space.

conjectures.label: middle-facet
conjectures.statement:
Under Assumptions~\ref{ass:randomization}--\ref{ass:witness-cell}, the certificate \(C_{\mathrm{mid}}(q;\Gamma):q_b^1\le T_{\mathrm{mid}}(\Gamma)\) is a valid and irredundant facet of the projected common-law cell polytope for the endpoint coordinate \(q_b^1\).  The facet is supported by the normal \(n_{\mathrm{mid}}=e_b^1\), and its active face is attained by
\[
  q_b^0=\frac{1}{2\Gamma+1},\quad
  q_a^0=q_c^0=\frac{\Gamma}{2\Gamma+1},\quad
  q_b^1=\frac{4\Gamma-1}{3(2\Gamma+1)},\quad
  q_a^1=q_c^1=\frac{\Gamma+2}{3(2\Gamma+1)}.
\]
Moreover, for every finite \(\Gamma>1\), no pair of independent adjacent-edge optimizers attaining \(T_{\mathrm{pair}}(\Gamma)\) on both incident edges has a common middle coordinate in the symmetric witness cell, because the middle coordinate required by each edge exceeds the coherent facet endpoint by \(\Delta_{\mathrm{mid}}^{\mathrm{sym}}(\Gamma)>0\).

conjectures.label: phase
conjectures.statement:
Under Assumptions~\ref{ass:randomization}--\ref{ass:phase-neighborhood}, the symmetric K=3 witness has a non-PI strict-containment phase exactly for \(\Gamma>1\).  In that cell,
\[
  \Delta_{\mathrm{mid}}^{\mathrm{sym}}(\Gamma)
  =\frac{\Gamma+2}{3(\Gamma+1)(2\Gamma+1)}>0,
\]
and the active-basis identities imply
\[
  T_{\mathrm{pair}}(\Gamma)-T_{\mathrm{mid}}(\Gamma)
  =
  \Delta_{\mathrm{mid}}^{\mathrm{sym}}(\Gamma).
\]
Thus the incident pairwise endpoint is separated from the coherent common-law endpoint by a primitive slack computed from the K=3 cell, not by a phase variable defined as the endpoint difference.  The conjectural extension is that the same active basis remains valid on a relative-open neighborhood of the symmetric witness inside the margin-compatible parameter space.

supporting_theorems.label: parent-reduction
supporting_theorems.statement: Under Assumptions~\ref{ass:randomization}--\ref{ass:divergence}, the sharp set from \texttt{pid\_ordinal\_principal\_strata/v1} is the image of \(\mathcal Q(P,\pi,\Gamma)\) under the linear map \(q\mapsto\mu_{1g}(q)-\mu_{0g}(q)\).  Thus the present proposal reduces to the parent when the only reported object is \([L_g(P,\pi,\Gamma),U_g(P,\pi,\Gamma)]\).
supporting_theorems.proof_tex:
\begin{theorem}[Theorem 1: parent sharp set as common-law polytope]\label{thm:parent-reduction}
Under Assumptions~\ref{ass:randomization}--\ref{ass:divergence}, the sharp set from \texttt{pid\_ordinal\_principal\_strata/v1} is the image of \(\mathcal Q(P,\pi,\Gamma)\) under the linear map \(q\mapsto\mu_{1g}(q)-\mu_{0g}(q)\).  Thus the present proposal reduces to the parent when the only reported object is \([L_g(P,\pi,\Gamma),U_g(P,\pi,\Gamma)]\).
\end{theorem}
\begin{proof}
The parent sharp set is defined by latent laws on \((G,Y(0),Y(1),S(0),S(1),X,Z)\) that have observable margin \(P\), satisfy the supplied principal-score law \(\pi\), and obey the same adjacent divergence restriction.  Given any such latent law, define
\[
  q_{z,h}(x,y)=\Pr(G=h\mid Z=z,S=h_z,X=x,Y=y).
\]
By construction, these coordinates are nonnegative and sum to one over \(h\in A_z(k)\) in each observed cell \((z,k,x,y)\).  Bayes' rule and the identity \(\Pr(G=h\mid Z=z,S=k,X=x)=w_h^z(x)\) imply the calibration equations
\[
  \sum_{y\in\mathcal Y} q_{z,h}(x,y)\Pr(Y=y\mid Z=z,S=k,X=x)=w_h^z(x).
\]
Because the latent law is assumed to satisfy the same \(\Gamma\)-adjacent odds-ratio restriction, the induced posterior array also satisfies
\[
  \Gamma^{-1}q_{z,\ell}(x,y)w_h^z(x)
  \le q_{z,h}(x,y)w_\ell^z(x)
  \le \Gamma q_{z,\ell}(x,y)w_h^z(x)
\]
for every adjacent pair \(h\sim_z \ell\).  Hence \(q\in\mathcal Q(P,\pi,\Gamma)\).

For the target stratum \(g\), randomization and consistency give
\[
  p_g\mu_{zg}=\E[\one\{G=g\}Y(z)] = \E[\one\{Z=z,S=g_z,G=g\}Y/e_z].
\]
Conditioning on \((Z=z,S=g_z,X,Y)\) yields
\[
  p_g\mu_{zg}=\E_P[\one\{Z=z,S=g_z\}q_{z,g}(X,Y)Y/e_z]=p_g\mu_{zg}(q).
\]
Therefore every admissible latent law produces the value \(\mu_{1g}(q)-\mu_{0g}(q)\) for some \(q\in\mathcal Q(P,\pi,\Gamma)\), so the parent sharp set is contained in the image of \(\mathcal Q(P,\pi,\Gamma)\).

Conversely, fix any \(q\in\mathcal Q(P,\pi,\Gamma)\).  On every positive observed cell \((z,k,x)\) and every \(h\in A_z(k)\) with \(w_h^z(x)>0\), define the arm-specific conditional outcome law
\[
  m_{z,h,x}(y)=\frac{q_{z,h}(x,y)\Pr_P(Y=y\mid Z=z,S=k,X=x)}{w_h^z(x)}.
\]
The simplex and calibration constraints ensure that \(m_{z,h,x}(y)\ge 0\) and \(\sum_y m_{z,h,x}(y)=1\).  When \(w_h^z(x)=0\), set the corresponding conditional law arbitrarily because \(\Pr(G=h,X=x)=0\).  Now construct a latent law by drawing \((X,G)\) from the supplied principal-score distribution, drawing \(Y(z)\) from \(m_{z,G,x}\) conditional on \((G,X)\), coupling \((Y(0),Y(1))\) arbitrarily conditional on \((G,X)\), drawing randomized \(Z\), and setting \(S(z)=G_z\), \(S=S(Z)\), and \(Y=Y(Z)\).

A direct Bayes calculation shows that this constructed law has observed margin \(P\): summing \(\pi_h(x)m_{z,h,x}(y)\) over \(h\in A_z(k)\) gives
\[
  \Pr_P(S=k\mid Z=z,X=x)\Pr_P(Y=y\mid Z=z,S=k,X=x)
\]
because the posterior weights sum to one in each cell and calibrate back to \(w^z(x)\).  The same construction recovers the prescribed posterior array \(q\), hence preserves the adjacent divergence inequalities.  Under this latent law,
\[
  \mu_{zg}=\mu_{zg}(q),
\]
so every point in the image of \(\mathcal Q(P,\pi,\Gamma)\) is attainable by a latent law consistent with the parent restrictions.

Thus the parent sharp set and the image of \(\mathcal Q(P,\pi,\Gamma)\) coincide.  Taking the minimum and maximum of the linear image recovers the parent interval \([L_g(P,\pi,\Gamma),U_g(P,\pi,\Gamma)]\).  Therefore the present qid reduces to the parent whenever only that interval is reported.
\end{proof}
